case_number: Constitution Petitions led by C.P No.D-

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1602 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (Priority) 1. For orders on CMA No.5428/2024 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 3. For orders on CMA No.5429/2024 (151 CPC) 4. For orders on CMA No.5446/2024 (Condonation of delay) 5. For hearing of CMA No.6831/2023 (S/A) 6. For hearing of main case 26.09.2024 Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant Advocate General Sindh along with Munir Ahmed Jumani, Director General (DG) Agriculture Extension, Sindh at Hyderabad >>>>>>...<<<<<<< 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Deferred. 4. Deferred. 5. Dismissed as not pressed. 3. Through this application (CMA No.5429/2024) a review of this Court’s order dated 03.09.2024 has been sought by the Applicant. Learned AAG Sindh states that the respondents want to use this opportunity by advertising the posts of Field Assistants in Agriculture Extension (the Department) to allow other individuals to also participate in the process as envisaged by the said order, as per the understanding of the Department, the said order was only passed for consideration the cases of the petitioners which are 52 in number, but the said opportunity could also be used for filling the remaining posts, and since Court through its order dated 03.09.2024 has granted 30 days’ time to do the needful, which is not sufficient, particularly when 2 the Department is filling 500 posts this time be elongated. After hearing the matter at some length, learned AAG Sindh states that he will not press the review application only to that the period of 30 days be extended upto 90 days, as the Department is intending to fill all the vacant posts of Field Assistant through the orders of this Court.

Learned AAG Sindh states that the respondents want to use this opportunity by advertising the posts of Field Assistants in Agriculture Extension (the Department) to allow other individuals to also participate in the process as envisaged by the said order, as per the understanding of the Department, the said order was only passed for consideration the cases of the petitioners which are 52 in number, but the said opportunity could also be used for filling the remaining posts, and since Court through its order dated 03.09.2024 has granted 30 days’ time to do the needful, which is not sufficient, particularly when 2 the Department is filling 500 posts this time be elongated. After hearing the matter at some length, learned AAG Sindh states that he will not press the review application only to that the period of 30 days be extended upto 90 days, as the Department is intending to fill all the vacant posts of Field Assistant through the orders of this Court. In the circumstances in the larger interest of justice, the request of the learned AAG is allowed. Let the Department place advertisements to initiate process for filling of nearly 500 posts of Field Assistant in a transparent manner through third party testing agency as envisaged by this Court’s order dated 08.03.2023 passed in a number of Constitution Petitions led by C.P No.D- 1234 of 2011 in which process the cases of the present petitioners, whose testimonials have already been cleared will not be required to resubmit their testimonials afresh with the exception of petitioner No.2 in C. P. No. D-1234/2011, let the process be completed transparently within 90 days from today and let the list of all the final candidates selected including above detailed petitioners be submitted before this Court, before any appointment orders are issued. To come up on 08.10.2024, when the matter is already fixed. Judge Judge ARBROHI
